title: Reading - Part 1
instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.heading: Art Competition
questions.text: Design a poster about saving water at school. Entries should be colourful and easy to read from a distance. Please give your poster to the office by Friday afternoon.
questions.question: What does the poster ask students to do?

options.B: Create a clear poster on a school issue.
options.A: Paint the school office before Friday.
options.C: Write a long report about water.

questions.explanation: Students should design a readable poster about saving water.

questions.heading: Message from Lucas to Erin
questions.text: Erin, the teacher says our presentation is first tomorrow, not last. We should practise at lunchtime because there won’t be time after school. Can you bring the notes?
questions.question: Why is Lucas texting Erin?

options.C: To arrange earlier practice for their presentation.
options.A: To say their presentation is no longer tomorrow.
options.B: To ask her to write new notes.

questions.explanation: Their presentation is first, so Lucas suggests practising at lunchtime.

questions.heading: Library Returns
questions.text: Books returned through the outside box after 5 p.m. will be checked the next morning. If your book is due today, bring it inside before closing time to avoid a late mark.
questions.question: What does the notice warn readers?

options.A: Late-day returns may not be recorded immediately.
options.B: The outside box is closed every morning.
options.C: Books cannot be returned inside the library.

questions.explanation: Books put in the box after 5 p.m. are checked the next morning.

questions.heading: From: Coach Davis / Subject: Training
questions.text: Tomorrow’s training will focus on passing, so please watch the short video I sent before you arrive. We will not have time to explain the exercise fully at the start.
questions.question: What should players do before training?

options.B: Prepare by watching the video carefully.
options.A: Explain the passing exercise to everyone.
options.C: Arrive late because training is shorter.

questions.explanation: Players should watch the video before arriving.

questions.heading: Local History Exhibition
questions.text: See photographs of the town from fifty years ago. Visitors can listen to recordings of local people talking about school, shops and transport. The exhibition is free for students.
questions.question: What can students do at the exhibition?

options.C: Learn about the town through pictures and voices.
options.A: Record new interviews with local people.
options.B: Buy old photographs of their school.

questions.explanation: The exhibition includes old photographs and recordings.
